skill.name: cloudformation-best-practices
skill.description: CloudFormation template optimization, nested stacks, drift detection, and production-ready patterns. Use when writing or reviewing CF templates.
skill.summary: Expert guidance for writing, reviewing, and optimizing AWS CloudFormation templates with production-ready patterns and best practices.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 22 static analysis findings are false positives. The flagged 'external_commands' are CloudFormation best practice instructions, 'network' is a VPC CIDR block parameter, 'C2 keywords' is a resource type name (VPC), and 'weak cryptographic algorithm' references are common English words. This is a legitimate infrastructure-as-code skill with no security concerns.

content.value_statement: CloudFormation templates can become complex and hard to maintain. This skill provides expert guidance on writing optimized, production-ready templates using nested stacks, drift detection, and proven patterns.
actual_capabilities: Review and optimize existing CloudFormation templates for readability and maintainability
Design nested stack architectures for modular infrastructure
Implement drift detection and troubleshooting for stack issues
Apply production-ready patterns including DeletionPolicy and Conditions
Validate templates using AWS CLI and cfn-lint tools
Create cross-stack references using Outputs and Import values
limitations: Does not generate complete infrastructure from scratch - provides guidance and best practices
Cannot execute CloudFormation operations or deploy stacks
Focuses on CloudFormation only, not CDK or Terraform
Does not provide real-time AWS resource status or pricing information

use_cases.title: Template Code Review
use_cases.description: Get expert feedback on your CloudFormation templates with recommendations for improvements, security hardening, and cost optimization.

use_cases.title: Architecture Design
use_cases.description: Design modular, scalable infrastructure using nested stacks, cross-stack references, and proven AWS patterns.

use_cases.title: Troubleshooting Support
use_cases.description: Resolve stack deployment failures, drift detection issues, and update rollback problems with step-by-step guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Template Review
prompt_templates.prompt:
Review this CloudFormation template for best practices and suggest improvements:

```yaml
# Your template here
```
prompt_templates.scenario: When user shares a template for review

prompt_templates.title: Nested Stack Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a nested stack architecture for a three-tier application with VPC, ECS clusters, and RDS databases.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user needs to organize complex infrastructure

prompt_templates.title: Drift Detection
prompt_templates.prompt: My CloudFormation stack shows drift. How do I identify the drifted resources and bring them back to the desired state?

prompt_templates.title: Production Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: What best practices should I apply to make this CloudFormation template production-ready? Include DeletionPolicy, Conditions, and cost optimization tips.
prompt_templates.scenario: When preparing templates for production deployment

output_examples.input: Review this template and suggest improvements for production use
output: 1. Add DeletionPolicy: Retain to RDS and DynamoDB resources
2. Use Parameters for environment-specific values instead of hardcoding
3. Add Outputs with Export for cross-stack references
4. Enable termination protection for production stacks

output_examples.input: How do I fix UPDATE_ROLLBACK_FAILED status?
output: 1. Run: aws cloudformation continue-update-rollback --stack-name STACK_NAME --resources-to-skip RESOURCE_ID
2. Identify the root cause using describe-stack-events
3. Fix the failing resource manually or update the template
5. Consider using stack policies to prevent accidental updates

best_practices: Use YAML over JSON for better readability and commenting support
Always set DeletionPolicy: Retain on stateful resources like RDS, S3, and DynamoDB to prevent data loss
Parameterize environment-specific values and use Mappings for static regional lookups
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode ARNs, account IDs, or region-specific values - use Fn::Sub with AWS pseudo parameters
Avoid monolithic single templates - decompose into nested stacks for better maintainability
Do not skip template validation - always run cfn-lint and aws cloudformation validate-template before deployment

faq.question: What is the difference between CloudFormation and CDK?
faq.answer: CloudFormation uses YAML or JSON templates to define infrastructure declaratively. CDK (Cloud Development Kit) uses programming languages like TypeScript or Python to generate CloudFormation templates. Choose CloudFormation for transparency and CDK for productivity.

faq.question: How do I handle secrets in CloudFormation?
faq.answer: Never hardcode secrets in templates. Use AWS Secrets Manager or Systems Manager Parameter Store with dynamic references. Example: {{resolve:secretsmanager:secret-id:SecretString:password}}

faq.question: What is drift detection in CloudFormation?
faq.answer: Drift detection compares the current stack resource configuration with the expected CloudFormation template. It identifies resources that were modified outside of CloudFormation, helping you maintain infrastructure consistency.

faq.question: When should I use nested stacks?
faq.answer: Use nested stacks when you have reusable infrastructure components like VPC configurations, common networking patterns, or shared application stacks. Nested stacks promote reusability and reduce template duplication.

faq.question: How do I update a stack without causing downtime?
faq.answer: Use change sets to preview updates before applying. Implement proper rollback mechanisms. Use UpdateReplacePolicy for resources that must be replaced. Test changes in a staging environment first.

faq.question: What tools integrate with CloudFormation for validation?
faq.answer: Use cfn-lint for syntax validation, cfn-nag for security scanning, and aws cloudformation validate-template for basic validation. Integrate these tools into your CI/CD pipeline for automated checks.
